delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: Students will use the Question Formation Technique to develop the important topics for reading and small-group discussion. The instructor will then develop appropriate reading and listening materials to be used both in-class discussions and as homework.
aims: This course aims to help students use English to understand internationally significant topics and issues. It also helps students develop their sense of being a citizen of the world and taking part in active citizenship. This class promotes student autonomy through English-language discourse, emphasizing the importance of inclusive and quality education, empowerment, equality, and collaboration for sustainable development.
goals: To understand the main points of English newspaper and magazine articles adapted for educational purposes.
To exchange opinions and feelings, express agreement and disagreement, and compare things and people using simple English.
To promote student engagement with global issues.
To develop media literacy and foster awareness of online mis- and disinformation.

plan.text: Introduction to Global Issues, Brainstorming, Question Formation Technique, Class Topic Development, Topic 1 Question Development

textbooks: All material will be selected by the instructor as events unfold during the semester.
prereq: Students should be able to understand the main points of newspaper articles that have been adapted for educational purposes and be able to express their ideas and opinions using basic everyday vocabulary and expressions. [教育用にアレンジされた新聞記事の要点を理解し、日常の基本的な語彙や表現を使って、自分の考えや意見を表現できるようになること。]
prep: This is a "flipped class" which means students must do the assignments before the class in order to participate fully in the class discussions.
teachers: ◎ デビット レスリー
